title: Microsoft Build opening keynote
sortTitle: microsoft build opening keynote
description: Satya Nadella and Microsoft leaders share how Microsoft is creating new opportunity for developers across our platforms in this era of AI.

title: Agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
sortTitle: agent supervision is the new senior engineering skill
description: As AI agents start shipping code, the bottleneck becomes trust. This talk argues “agent supervision” is the new senior skill: scoping agent work, setting constraints, designing checkpoints, and reviewing outputs for correctness and security. Learn practical patterns to catch silent failures and build workflows where humans stay accountable and agents stay fast.

title: AI & Agent-Augmented coding you can trust
sortTitle: ai & agent-augmented coding you can trust
description: Agents now run real commands, modify files, and move data. This breakout highlights the full set of Windows primitives that help agents discover, reason about, and execute AI‑powered activities across the system, while still operating within clear boundaries. Through live demos, you’ll see how agents identify available capabilities, plan and take meaningful actions, and how Windows layers permission scoping, inspection, developer tool capabilities, and rollback to keep developers in control.

title: Aspire for agents: Transform how you build and deploy distributed apps
sortTitle: aspire for agents: transform how you build and deploy distributed apps
description: Aspire is an open-source cloud and agent-ready developer toolchain transforming how you build, run, and deploy observable, production-ready distributed apps. Its code centric approach streamlines orchestration of your apps and agents, provides observability for systems in any programming language, and supports deployment locally or to your cloud of choice. Come meet the new Aspire.

title: Azure DevOps meets GitHub, the path to AI powered SDLC
sortTitle: azure devops meets github, the path to ai powered sdlc
description: Azure DevOps and GitHub are better together—and the integration keeps getting smarter. In this demo-heavy session, you’ll see how hybrid patterns that connect GitHub with Azure Boards and Azure Pipelines enable Agentic DevOps. See the all of the newest AI-powered capabilities in Azure DevOps. Plus, hear how Microsoft's engineering teams adopted this approach and what they gained.

title: Breakout session with Peter Steinberger
sortTitle: breakout session with peter steinberger
description: To be announced closer to the event.

title: Build and host enterprise-grade AI agents with Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: build and host enterprise-grade ai agents with microsoft foundry
description: AI agents are transforming how developers build software—but shipping production-grade agents demands more. This session walks through the end-to-end lifecycle of building AI agents with Foundry Agent Service and Microsoft Agent Framework. See how to go from local prototyping to enterprise-grade hosted deployment with identity, secure networking, evaluations, and lifecycle management. Learn how coding agents like GitHub Copilot integrate directly into the workflow.

title: Build and ship faster with a developer-optimized experience on Windows
sortTitle: build and ship faster with a developer-optimized experience on windows
description: Learn how Windows delivers a streamlined, end-to-end experience through PCs and OS experiences optimized for developers. See our new experiences in action across WSL, PowerToys and your favorite developer tools so you can code with less toil and stay in your workflows. Walk away with repeatable scenarios that you can easily integrate in your daily development workflows, helping you scale your AI projects with confidence.

title: Build context‑aware agents at scale with Microsoft IQ
sortTitle: build context‑aware agents at scale with microsoft iq
description: High performance agents are built on intelligence that brings together context, enterprise data, orchestration, and governance. Learn how Foundry IQ, Fabric IQ, and Work IQ provide the enterprise intelligence layer for AI agents. Design agents that can search across organizational knowledge, reason over business data, and operate with awareness of people and work signals. Take action within trusted boundaries, providing a practical foundation for building scalable and reliable agents.

title: Build local AI powered experiences w/ Microsoft Foundry on Windows
sortTitle: build local ai powered experiences w/ microsoft foundry on windows
description: Start with solution-centric Windows AI APIs - now expanding beyond NPU to support GPU and CPU. Use Foundry Local to run open-source models locally. With new tooling for VS Code, optimize and prep your models for on-device deployment. Run your custom AI workloads locally across GPU, NPU, or CPU with Windows ML, now supporting web apps through WebNN. Learn how the platform enables on-device inference across all silicon to help you ship performant, scalable, and secure AI-powered Windows apps.

title: Claw agents and multi-agent systems on Foundry
sortTitle: claw agents and multi-agent systems on foundry
description: Go deep on multi-agent systems built on Microsoft Foundry, featuring Claw agent patterns and the hosted agents architecture. Explore long-running agents with triggers, state management, and file access—all natively supported on Foundry. See how coding agents built with GitHub Copilot SDK and Claude Agent SDK integrate into multi-agent workflows using Microsoft Agent Framework. Learn how to coordinate, host, and operate these systems with observability and continuous evals.

title: Confident model selection and integration with Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: confident model selection and integration with microsoft foundry
description: Discover how to quickly choose, integrate, and validate AI models inside Microsoft Foundry. Learn techniques for navigating thousands of model options, benchmarking performance, and streamlining your workflow with deep IDE support. Build faster, ship smarter, and stay on top of the evolving AI landscape.

title: Data, apps, and agents: the future of app dev with Microsoft Fabric
sortTitle: data, apps, and agents: the future of app dev with microsoft fabric
description: Apps are coming to the data platform, built for the new era of agent development. Join us to see how Fabric makes it easy to build secure, shareable, agent-powered apps that connect natively to your existing data and integrate seamlessly with your AI and BI workflows. By bringing together essential data platform capabilities, Fabric reduces backend complexity so teams can move faster from idea to impact.

title: Deploy. Observe. Learn. Reinforcement learning for production agents
sortTitle: deploy. observe. learn. reinforcement learning for production agents
description: Agents don’t fail in demos. They fail in production, where accuracy, latency, cost, and reliability are make or break. In this breakout, we show how teams use fine tuning and reinforcement learning on Microsoft Foundry to continuously improve production agents using real usage signals. We focus on model level improvement, when fine tuning reduces cost and latency, when reinforcement learning delivers deeper gains, and how Foundry makes it easy to safely train, evaluate, and redeploy models.

title: Distributed systems to AI platforms with Mark Russinovich & Ion Stoica
sortTitle: distributed systems to ai platforms with mark russinovich & ion stoica
description: What will it take to build AI platforms for the agent era? Join Azure CTO Mark Russinovich and UC Berkeley professor Ion Stoica (co-creator of Apache Spark and co-founder of Databricks and Anyscale) to explore how AI infrastructure must evolve as systems become agentic, multimodal, and globally distributed. Get practical insights on next generation architectures, from training to real time serving, and why open source, security, and governance are now core platform concerns.

title: Ensuring your code works when AI testing isn’t enough
sortTitle: ensuring your code works when ai testing isn’t enough
description: Technical talk about building AI systems that scale for humans and machines, ensuring reliable code, and scalable systems.

title: Foundry IQ: Fuel your agents with knowledge from everywhere
sortTitle: foundry iq: fuel your agents with knowledge from everywhere
description: Foundry IQ is Microsoft's context engineering platform that enables agents to unlock knowledge from everywhere, with state-of-the-art agentic RAG and enterprise-grade security built in. In this technical deep dive, we share the latest developments in Foundry IQ and Azure AI Search, new knowledge sources, agentic retrieval enhancements, and present retrieval performance innovations and evals for our 3rd year in a row — all designed to help you ship higher-quality agents faster.

title: From CLI to PR: Automating the path to merged code
sortTitle: from cli to pr: automating the path to merged code
description: Everyone talks about agents, but the real challenge is applying them to daily sprints. Moving beyond chat, we'll show how GitHub Copilot functions as an agentic partner in your workflow by live-coding a full cycle—from planning in the terminal to delegating work to the cloud and automating PR reviews. No high-level abstractions here. Just technical mechanics: context management, advanced features with Copilot CLI, and the patterns that make agentic workflows actually stick.

title: From rows to reasoning: Designing databases for AI apps and agents
sortTitle: from rows to reasoning: designing databases for ai apps and agents
description: AI apps and agents need data platforms built for reasoning, not just transactions. Traditional architectures force developers to stitch together databases, analytics, and vector systems - adding latency and complexity. In this session, we'll demo how Azure databases bring transactional, analytical, vector, and real‑time data together to power AI‑ready apps. Reduce data movement, simplify architecture, and enable apps to reason over live data.

title: From Skeptic to Superpower: Real‑World AI Coding Workflows That Scale
sortTitle: from skeptic to superpower: real‑world ai coding workflows that scale
description: AI coding tools promise massive productivity gains, but which ones actually work and why? In this session, we’ll walk through how two engineers use different tools daily: one using Claude Code + Cursor, the other GitHub Copilot CLI. We’ll cover what initially didn’t work, skepticism we had, where each tool shines, and where they fall down. We’ll show what this looks like for a team of 20 engineers that delivers at a scale of 200. No hype, just realistic workflows, tradeoffs, and lessons learned.

title: Get the right tools for your agents with 1-click
sortTitle: get the right tools for your agents with 1-click
description: Tired of searching for the right tool(s) for your agents? Seeing your context window constantly blowing out of proportion? We are introducing a new product category that allows developers to choose the right toolset depending upon the agents' context, the use-case and functional boundaries, enabling efficient use of tools, with built-in governance.

title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio: Agents That Debug, Profile, and Test
sortTitle: github copilot in visual studio: agents that debug, profile, and test
description: Every IDE has agentic code generation now, but Visual Studio goes further with built-in agents that tackle what code generation can't. In this demo-heavy session, you'll see agents root-cause bugs using live runtime behavior, pinpoint performance bottlenecks and recommend targeted fixes, and build test coverage to catch regressions before they ship. These aren't just code-writing agents. They help enterprise C#, .NET, and C++ developers improve code quality with expert-level diagnostics.

title: Govern open-source AI agents, any framework, any scale
sortTitle: govern open-source ai agents, any framework, any scale
description: As AI agents move into production, developers own safety, governance, and reliability across Microsoft Agent Framework and open-source stacks. This session shows how to govern agents end to end: turning your requirements into context-aware evaluations, stress-testing against adversarial risks, applying open controls that work across frameworks, and keeping humans in the loop on high-stakes actions. Leave with a blueprint for shipping agents at enterprise scale.

title: Idea to production-ready agent in seconds on AI-native runtime
sortTitle: idea to production-ready agent in seconds on ai-native runtime
description: Agentic apps behave differently from traditional services. They make decisions, coordinate steps, and react to changing inputs. This session shows how to run agentic workloads on a fast, AI native runtime on Azure Container Apps. The focus is on reliability and speed so you can move from idea to production‑ready agents. We’ll cover patterns for deployment, configuration, safe scaling for bursty traffic, and observability so teams can run agentic systems in production without surprises.

title: Inside Azure innovations with Mark Russinovich
sortTitle: inside azure innovations with mark russinovich
description: Join Mark Russinovich, CTO, Deputy CISO, Technical Fellow of Microsoft Azure. Mark will take you on a tour of the latest innovations in Azure architecture and explain how Azure enables intelligent, modern, and innovative applications at scale in the cloud, on‑premises, and on the edge.

title: Local Models, Developer Control, and the Future of AI Runtimes
sortTitle: local models, developer control, and the future of ai runtimes
description: How local and hybrid model execution is reshaping developer workflows, privacy, and experimentation. Why “run it yourself” is back.

title: Mark and Scott learn...the future of software engineering
sortTitle: mark and scott learn...the future of software engineering
description: AI makes us faster. But does it make us better? We’ll explore how modern tooling is reshaping learning, why early-career engineers are at risk of being left behind, and what a new model of preceptorship might look like. Along the way, we’ll show real examples of AI helping build genuinely cool projects plus the failures, mistakes, and near-misses that come with it. Real talk, no hype.

title: Modern resiliency from build to recovery through Agentic AI
sortTitle: modern resiliency from build to recovery through agentic ai
description: Resiliency failures rarely come from missing features; they stem from fragmentation. During incidents teams lose time jumping between repos, dashboards, runbooks, and chat tools. This session reframes resiliency as an agent-first practice spanning build, operate, troubleshoot, and recovery. See how an agentic AI assistant links workflows across IDEs, source repos, and collaboration tools speeding validation, operations, and recovery for a critical workload aligned with Azure’s agentic model.

title: Multi-agent patterns in VS Code you won't learn from docs
sortTitle: multi-agent patterns in vs code you won't learn from docs
description: Building with one agent is familiar. Orchestrating a fleet of them in parallel across local, background, and cloud surfaces is where it gets real. You'll see the decisions that matter, how to decompose work across agents, when to fork vs. delegate, and how to verify quality when agents outnumber you. Leave with patterns for multi-agent workflows you can apply to your own codebase tomorrow.

title: Observability for AI agents on any framework
sortTitle: observability for ai agents on any framework
description: Nondeterministic, multi-agent systems break traditional monitoring. As agents move into production, developers need observability built into the workflow, not bolted on after failures. This session explores what modern observability looks like for AI agents: how tracing and evaluation must work across any framework, why the inner loop deserves the same rigor as the outer, how context-specific evals evolve with your agent, and how always-on signals tie behavior to business outcomes.

title: Shipping custom models at scale from fine-tuning to inference
sortTitle: shipping custom models at scale from fine-tuning to inference
description: Fine tuning, serving, and inference are core components of real world AI adoption. In this panel Rob Ferguson, Daniel Han (Unsloth), Mark Saroufim (Stealth Startup) and other practitioners discuss how teams are customizing models, taking them to production, and running them efficiently at scale. The conversation covers tradeoffs in fine tuning techniques, infrastructure choices for serving, optimizing inference cost and latency, and what’s working today for builders shipping AI powered products.

title: The honest practitioner's take on agentic AI on Kubernetes
sortTitle: the honest practitioner's take on agentic ai on kubernetes
description: Agentic AI workloads don't behave like normal services. They're stateful, bursty, multi-step, and often span more than a single cluster. Most teams figure this out the hard way. This session is for developers building agentic AI in production. We'll show you how to get the most out of Kubernetes for training, inference, and agent orchestration, covering purpose-built tooling, managed options, open-source inference at scale, and AI-assisted dev tools that support real-world AI operations.

title: Thirsty for more data: how Pepsi refreshed for agentic apps
sortTitle: thirsty for more data: how pepsi refreshed for agentic apps
description: Learn from Microsoft and PepsiCo engineers who modernized PepsiCo’s data foundation for agentic applications using Azure SQL, Cosmos DB, PostgreSQL, and Azure Databricks. Discover a practical build path for agentic RAG architecture, leveraging Azure SQL features like vector indexing and semantic search, to streamline app development and enable faster, repeatable patterns.  Refresh your own data layer to reduce app development cycles while leveraging modern, repeatable patterns to ship faster.

title: Train and deploy custom OSS reasoning models with Foundry
sortTitle: train and deploy custom oss reasoning models with foundry
description: Open‑source reasoning models work out of the box, but production requires domain‑specific training. In this session, ML engineers will learn how to use Microsoft Foundry to train and tune open‑source reasoning models in a code‑first workflow using curated RL environments and modern frameworks. We’ll cover when RL drives real gains in reasoning, how to run reproducible training loops, and how to move from experiment to production deployment without managing infrastructure.

title: Using AI tools to teach old apps new tricks
sortTitle: using ai tools to teach old apps new tricks
description: Modernizing apps isn’t just rewriting code—it’s untangling dependencies, tracing data flows, and making changes without breaking production. In this session, we’ll show how you can use agentic AI to take on the hardest parts of modernization: analyzing large codebases, mapping dependencies, planning upgrades, refactoring safely, while doing it all at scale. With GitHub Copilot modernization capabilities, you can move from legacy complexity to modernized apps in days, not months.

title: What to build next when AI can build everything
sortTitle: what to build next when ai can build everything
description: Practical guidance on thinking through what to build that can be differentiated and defensible in the era of AI.

title: What we learned shipping VS Code weekly (without breaking everything)
sortTitle: what we learned shipping vs code weekly (without breaking everything)
description: Shipping faster sounds great until test gaps, review bottlenecks, and triage backlog scale with it. The VS Code team hit all of that going from monthly to weekly releases, and agents made it work. This session breaks down the real patterns: agent sessions before meetings, conversations that become PRs instead of specs, automated triage across one of GitHub's largest repos, and harnesses that keep quality high when 100+ commits land daily. Concrete workflows you can take back to your team.

title: Why your AI code doesn’t ship: Closing the gap to production
sortTitle: why your ai code doesn’t ship: closing the gap to production
description: AI isn't just autocompleting your code anymore — it's writing plans, shipping PRs, fixing pipelines, and patching prod. In this demo-heavy session, we'll show AI agents working across the entire dev lifecycle: planning, coding, CI/CD, and live operations. You'll see how to move faster, keep agents on a leash, and build systems that fix themselves.

title: Your agent, anywhere: MultiClient, MultiDevice with GitHub Copilot SDK
sortTitle: your agent, anywhere: multiclient, multidevice with github copilot sdk
description: Agents are powerful on your machine, but what happens when you need them everywhere else? In this session, we'll show how GitHub Copilot SDK lets you build an agent, embed it in an app, and take it with you across devices and into the cloud. You'll see how to go from a local agent to one you can access on your phone, move between machines, and run across multiple clients. If you've been working with agents locally and wondering what the next step looks like, this is it.

title: Build an AI app with Azure SQL Hyperscale, Microsoft Fabric, & Foundry
sortTitle: build an ai app with azure sql hyperscale, microsoft fabric, & foundry
description: Build an AI-powered FAQ assistant using Azure SQL Database vector search and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with Azure OpenAI Service. Use GitHub Copilot to speed T-SQL, mirror data into Microsoft Fabric (OneLake) for analytics, apply governance with Microsoft Purview, and orchestrate the workflow with Microsoft Foundry Agents.

title: Build your knowledge base with Foundry IQ, query it from Copilot CLI
sortTitle: build your knowledge base with foundry iq, query it from copilot cli
description: In this lab, you'll build a knowledge base in Foundry IQ using the Microsoft Foundry portal, then expose it as an MCP server and connect it to GitHub Copilot CLI. Along the way, you’ll see how Foundry IQ brings together knowledge from across the Microsoft IQ intelligence layer to deliver unified, agent‑ready context. By the end, you'll see how Foundry IQ and Copilot CLI work together to bring your knowledge base directly into your developer workflow.

title: Build, deploy, and scale agents with Windows 365
sortTitle: build, deploy, and scale agents with windows 365
description: Learn how Windows 365 delivers secure and preconfigured computing environments for AI agents, and how you can combine that with computer use to accelerate the creation and deployment of AI agents in enterprise environments. Through technical demos, this session shows how to set up Cloud PCs and how agentic AI interacts with live apps and UIs, while keeping governance, identity, and enterprise controls built in.

title: Create advanced Postgres-powered agentic apps with Azure HorizonDB
sortTitle: create advanced postgres-powered agentic apps with azure horizondb
description: In this lab, you’ll build a production-ready AI agent using Azure HorizonDB as a unified data, retrieval, and intelligence layer. Implement RAG, hybrid semantic and keyword search, and agentic workflows without stitching external services. Use DiskANN advanced filtering and in-database AI model management to run LLMs and embeddings directly in the database - no vector stores or glue code. Ideal for developers and architects who want simpler stacks, higher performance, and faster AI delivery.

title: Engineering agents that reason, act, and adapt
sortTitle: engineering agents that reason, act, and adapt
description: Lost in San Francisco with a deadline to make it to Build, you’ll code an AI agent using Foundry Agent Service, Foundry IQ, and Foundry Tools. Step by step, you’ll design an agent that reasons, acts, and adapts in real time—integrating MCP tools to solve challenges along the way. Leave with hands‑on experience building and orchestrating production‑ready agentic systems.

title: From zero to deployed on Azure with AI agents
sortTitle: from zero to deployed on azure with ai agents
description: What happens when you let AI agents do the building? In this hands-on lab, you'll go from an empty terminal to a deployed app on Azure — with GitHub Copilot CLI and coding agents handling the scaffolding, coding, debugging, and deployment. You'll use the new Azure skills to provision resources and wire up services through natural language, no portal required. This isn't a demo you watch. You'll walk out with a real, working dev workflow you can take straight to your next project.

title: Hands-on with Azure Cobalt 200 – from code to cloud-native deployment
sortTitle: hands-on with azure cobalt 200 – from code to cloud-native deployment
description: Roll up your sleeves and get hands-on with Azure Cobalt 200. In this lab you'll provision your first Cobalt 200 VM and explore what makes Arm tick in the Cloud. From there you'll build multi-arch container images with Docker, push them to ACR, and deploy to AKS clusters running on Cobalt 200. Next, wire up a CI/CD pipeline that builds, tests, and deploys to Arm. Then go further—deploy a full-stack web app and serve AI predictions using ONNX Runtime on Arm. Walk out ready to build on Cobalt 200.

title: Make GitHub Copilot Work Your Way: Custom Tools, Context and Workflows
sortTitle: make github copilot work your way: custom tools, context and workflows
description: GitHub Copilot is powerful out of the box, but custom agents let you shape it to fit your team's exact workflows. In this lab, you'll build agents from scratch: set their roles, write instructions, scope their tools, and plug them into real workflows. You'll create reusable Agent Skills for domain knowledge and connect agents to external services via MCP. Everything works across VS Code, Visual Studio, CLI, and Copilot coding agent. Leave with working agents you can adapt to your own codebase.

title: Observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: observe, optimize and protect your hosted agents in microsoft foundry
description: Modern agents fail in ways traditional monitoring can’t catch. In this hands-on lab, learn how Microsoft Foundry Observability helps you move from prototype → production with context-specific evaluation suites (auto-generated evaluators + test datasets) wired into developer workflows via skills/MCP tooling for hosted agents. Then scale quality with continuous evaluation, trace-linked analysis, and adaptive red teaming—and walk away with a sandbox to explore additional features on your own.

title: Take LLMs from prototype to production on AKS
sortTitle: take llms from prototype to production on aks
description: Moving an AI model from experiment to production is hard. Learn about AIRunway, an open-source accelerator that simplifies deploying LLMs on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS). By treating models as native Kubernetes resources, AIRunway offers a single interface that adapts to multiple inference backends. You’ll deploy a production LLM on AKS, implement custom resources for scaling and networking, configure GPU and latency monitoring, and integrate it into CI/CD pipelines.

title: Terminal to Party: Live-Coding with GitHub Copilot CLI and Hydra
sortTitle: terminal to party: live-coding with github copilot cli and hydra
description: Experience GitHub Copilot CLI while exploring OSS Hydra, a live-coding audio/visual environment that runs directly in the browser. Learn how to use agentic loops to create an AV party of your very own. EDM? Lo-fi? Have it all! You'll find your groove while exploring all that Copilot CLI has to offer, from planning, to managing multiple agents, to delegating longer tasks to the cloud. What better way to learn - and discover your new dev soundtrack!

title: Use agentic tools in Copilot Studio
sortTitle: use agentic tools in copilot studio
description: Join us for a hands‑on learning experience and see how Frontier organizations are using Work IQ MCP tools and Skills to power agentic experiences at enterprise scale. In this lab, you’ll explore how agents are grounded in real‑time organizational context and transform that insight into consistent, reusable actions across workflows and applications—moving beyond isolated prompts to deliver confident, governed outcomes.

title: Any agent, any cloud: Microsoft Foundry + OpenTelemetry tracing
sortTitle: any agent, any cloud: microsoft foundry + opentelemetry tracing
description: Your teams are shipping agents across clouds and frameworks, but telemetry becomes schema soup. In this demo, we’ll show how to standardize agent observability using Microsoft Foundry and OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions across prompt, hosted, and custom agents. We’ll walk the trace → insight → eval loop to instrument model and tool calls, surface failures, latency regressions, and cost spikes, then close the loop with trace‑based evaluations and visualizations.

title: Build and deploy an Azure app with your agent team
sortTitle: build and deploy an azure app with your agent team
description: In 25 minutes, we’ll go from a blank repo to a live Azure AI app—built and deployed by a team of GitHub Copilot agents. Watch Copilot plan architecture, generate code, provision Azure, and deploy live using Copilot Agents and Skills. Leave with a blueprint for your agent driven coding workflow for Azure.

title: Build and ship production-ready apps fast with Microsoft Fabric
sortTitle: build and ship production-ready apps fast with microsoft fabric
description: Build an enterprise app in minutes with an agent-friendly, code-first backend-as-a-service built on Microsoft Fabric. In this demo, we show how an AI agent generates a full stack web app with a managed database, authentication, and backend services. Learn how to connect the app to existing data in your Fabric data estate, define services in code, and use Fabric’s built-in analytics, BI, and AI capabilities to power insights and intelligent experiences.

title: Code a voice-first agent with avatars in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: code a voice-first agent with avatars in microsoft foundry
description: Build a real-time agent with a conversational avatar using the Voice Live API in Microsoft Foundry, no slides, just live coding. See how Voice Live unifies speech-to-text, model reasoning, and text-to-speech, then wire it into an agent that can call tools via MCP to take real actions. Walk away with reusable patterns for voice-first, tool-using agents with expressive avatars.

title: Fine‑tuning isn’t first: smarter training in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: fine‑tuning isn’t first: smarter training in microsoft foundry
description: Training doesn’t start—or end—with the model. In Microsoft Foundry, teams are improving agent performance by closing the loop between model selection, orchestration, evaluation, and iteration. This lightning talk walks through how Foundry enables faster experimentation across models, prompts, and agent workflows, while maintaining observability and cost control. Learn how teams move from promising demos to production‑ready agents using Foundry’s integrated training and evaluation signals.

title: From chatbots to work-completing agents: Building with the Teams SDK
sortTitle: from chatbots to work-completing agents: building with the teams sdk
description:
Agents are evolving beyond simple chat interactions. With the Teams SDK, developers can build intelligent agents that integrate directly into Teams experiences and help users get real work done.

This session will show how the Teams SDK unlocks rich integrations with Teams features and AI services, enabling agents that automate workflows and take action across Teams surfaces. Learn how to build agents that accelerate productivity and deliver value without ever leaving Teams.

title: From prompt to app, build AI powered apps on Windows
sortTitle: from prompt to app, build ai powered apps on windows
description: From prompt to app, learn how to use GitHub Copilot CLI and skills to build an AI‑powered Windows application that takes advantage of local compute. Walk through an end‑to‑end workflow that combines native UI with hardware‑accelerated AI using Windows AI APIs and Windows ML while optimizing models for local performance using WinML Model kit. Bring your existing Linux codebase to Windows by packaging Linux containers running in WSL into a single installable Windows desktop app.

title: GitHub Copilot Anywhere: From CLIs to Cloud Execution Environments
sortTitle: github copilot anywhere: from clis to cloud execution environments
description: Modern development no longer happens in one place. You might start in your terminal, step away, and pick things back up from another device or let work continue in the cloud. This session explores using GitHub Copilot across environments, from local CLI sessions to full cloud execution with remote execution environments. See how Copilot runs commands, iterates on changes, and continues work without losing context, while you stay in control.

title: How Foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
sortTitle: how foundry integrates with open-source frameworks and tools
description: In this demo, we'll show how Microsoft Foundry integrates with the open-source tools developers already use — so you can build agents your way and deploy them with enterprise-grade hosting and observability built in. We'll start with Microsoft Agent Framework, then bring in a LangGraph agent and deploy it directly to Foundry with no rearchitecting required. MCP, A2A, and OpenAPI connect it all — letting agents built across different frameworks coordinate in multi-agent workflows.

title: Late to agentic coding? Don’t panic, build.
sortTitle: late to agentic coding? don’t panic, build.
description: ​Feeling behind on agentic coding? You’re not. In this demo-heavy session, we’ll show how to go from idea to shipped. You’ll see practical patterns for planning tasks, delegating implementation, reviewing AI-generated PRs, and shipping with guardrails. No hype - just real workflows you can apply with your team right away.

title: Multi-agents in action with 3 AI agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
sortTitle: multi-agents in action with 3 ai agents, 3 frameworks, tools & models
description: Three agent frameworks. One Agentic Startup Content Factory. Zero manual steps. In this live demo we build an intelligent system that researches topics, writes articles and code samples, and refines its own output, all while running autonomously. We'll deploy LangGraph, .NET Microsoft Agent Framework, and GitHub Copilot SDK agents to Azure Container Apps, then connect Microsoft Foundry for observability and evals. You'll walk out with an Agentic Startup Content Factory you can clone and ship.

title: Rethinking CI: Actions, AI Agents, and the End of Commit-Fail-Commit
sortTitle: rethinking ci: actions, ai agents, and the end of commit-fail-commit
description: Your pipelines automate the predictable. But what about triaging issues, reviewing PRs, responding to incidents, and coordinating across tools? See what's new in GitHub Actions and how it's becoming the execution layer for AI agents across your dev lifecycle. We'll cover agent-triggered workflows, MCP server integration, and automated handoffs that keep humans in the loop — plus how to finally break the 'commit - see CI fail - commit again' loop.

title: Scale cloud-native workloads with Azure Linux
sortTitle: scale cloud-native workloads with azure linux
description: Azure Linux is a purpose-built Linux distribution optimized for Azure. Learn how Azure Linux supports cloud-native and AI workloads with deep integration into the Azure ecosystem, delivering a consistent Linux platform across containers and other Azure compute services. Designed with a minimal footprint, Azure Linux enables faster provisioning and scaling, reduces the attack surface, and incorporates Azure's robust cloud security standards.

title: Ship code faster with AI-powered NoSQL schema design
sortTitle: ship code faster with ai-powered nosql schema design
description: NoSQL schema design is hard—denormalization decisions, partition key selection, and data modeling patterns require expertise. Use GitHub Copilot and the new Azure Cosmos DB Agent Toolkit to accelerate development with AI-assisted schema generation, query optimization suggestions, and refactoring recommendations. Iterate rapidly with the new Mac/Linux emulator for local testing. Demo shows schema evolution across three iterations in 30 minutes versus days of manual design.

title: Smaller, faster, smarter: Distilling agents with fine‑tuning
sortTitle: smaller, faster, smarter: distilling agents with fine‑tuning
description: Large models are powerful, but expensive to run in production. In this lightning talk, we’ll show how teams use Foundry for distillation and supervised fine-tuning to train small language models for task‑specific accuracy, dramatically reducing latency and cost. We’ll cover when distillation makes sense, how it complements fine tuning and reinforcement learning, and what real production teams have learned when deploying smaller models at scale. Expect fast examples and lots of Q&A.

title: Supercharged profiling: Finding performance bugs with agents
sortTitle: supercharged profiling: finding performance bugs with agents
description: Modern performance problems are harder than ever to diagnose: distributed systems, async code, and massive data make traditional profiling insufficient. In this demo-driven session, watch how Visual Studio combines advanced diagnostics with AI-powered agents to surface bottlenecks, explain root causes, and guide fixes faster than ever. From CPU and memory issues to “it only fails in production” bugs, you’ll see how profiling evolves in an agentic world.

title: Turn APIs, tools, and data into real agent velocity
sortTitle: turn apis, tools, and data into real agent velocity
description: Most teams already have the APIs and data they need. In this demo, see how Foundry enables agents to securely call real APIs, use tools, and act on enterprise data. Learn how Foundry IQ provides grounding and context, while runtime governance ensures tool usage is observable and controlled—turning existing systems into callable agent capabilities.

title: Union types in C#
sortTitle: union types in c#
description: Union types are coming to C#! Unions model closed sets of data shapes, as commonly seen in e.g. wire protocols. Mads and Dustin explore the clean expression of intent and the confidence and elegance that unions lend to consuming code.

title: What's new in Windows Subsystem for Linux
sortTitle: what's new in windows subsystem for linux
description: Learn about new improvements coming to WSL enabling you to build AI-powered applications on Windows.

title: 10 ways to maximize GitHub Copilot
sortTitle: 10 ways to maximize github copilot
description: It started as autocomplete, but that's so 2024. What can GitHub Copilot really do in 2026? Join us for our 10 favorite hidden gems. Number will shock you!

title: Agentic RAG for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on Azure Cosmos DB
sortTitle: agentic rag for fast, low-cost knowledge retrieval on azure cosmos db
description: Skip the knowledge graph and ontology construction. DiverseRAG delivers high-quality answers to open-ended, multi-hop, and nuanced questions in seconds using GPU-accelerated local models and Azure Cosmos DB — combining hybrid full-text and vector search, diversity selection, and sub-question decomposition across large structured and unstructured datasets. You'll leave knowing how to build production-ready knowledge retrieval as long-term agentic memory to boost your agents' accuracy and speed.

title: Better tests, faster. GitHub Copilot does the heavy lifting
sortTitle: better tests, faster. github copilot does the heavy lifting
description: AI agents write code faster than ever — but quality still matters. In this demo, see how GitHub Copilot transforms your testing workflow — from generating test suites to TDD-style agent development, catching edge cases, and validating agent-generated code before it merges. From unit tests to load testing, whether retrofitting a legacy codebase or building greenfield, see the patterns that keep AI-assisted development reliable and production-ready.

title: Build and govern connected agents with Agent 365 + MCP
sortTitle: build and govern connected agents with agent 365 + mcp
description: In this lightning talk, attendees learn how to connect AI agents to real‑world work systems using Model Context Protocol (MCP), then bring those agents under Agent 365 management and observability. We’ll show how MCP enables agents to securely access tools and context, and how Agent 365 provides visibility, monitoring, and control as those agents run in production.

title: Building and deploying production-ready AI without the headaches
sortTitle: building and deploying production-ready ai without the headaches
description: Ready to launch your AI model? This session shows simple, code-first workflows for model creation, deploymentless runtime, and dynamic routing. See how policy controls, observability, and frictionless deployment features inside Foundry make scaling and governance easy, allowing you to move quickly from prototype to production.

title: Building end-to-end agentic automation in 15 mins
sortTitle: building end-to-end agentic automation in 15 mins
description: In just 15 minutes, see how Copilot Studio can deliver a complete autonomous agentic automation —from idea to deployment. Learn to use a combination of out of the box and your own custom MCPs, leverage Copilot Studio’s both goal-based automations like orchestration and CUA as well as rule-based automation capabilities like Agent Flows. This session reveals how to streamline tasks, reduce manual effort, and supercharge productivity for developers wanting to modernize their internal systems.

title: Change-driven AI agents with Drasi and LangChain
sortTitle: change-driven ai agents with drasi and langchain
description: Drasi is a change‑detection platform for databases that enables reactive, change‑driven AI agents. The LangChain‑Drasi integration connects real‑time data changes to agent workflows using continuous queries. Agents can discover and subscribe to changes, update state and memory, and adapt execution—turning static agents into long‑lived, responsive systems.

title: Debugging and Securing MCP Servers on Windows
sortTitle: debugging and securing mcp servers on windows
description:
This session demonstrates one complete MCP server implemented and run on Windows. Using live code, we expose a single Windows capability and trace how it is packaged, registered, trusted, and executed by the platform. 
You will learn how Windows enforces security and lifecycle boundaries, what responsibilities stay with the developer, and how to debug common failure modes such as denied access and misregistration. The walkthrough uses local tooling, Windows containers, and registry inspection.

title: Develop and scale without limits using Azure SQL Database Hyperscale
sortTitle: develop and scale without limits using azure sql database hyperscale
description: In this demo‑driven session, see how Azure SQL Database Hyperscale removes scaling limits without changing how you build. Using familiar SQL tools and patterns, we’ll start with a transactional app and show how Hyperscale delivers performance on demand, scales effortlessly as your app grows, and stays AI‑ready by design—so you can focus on writing code, shipping features faster, and building intelligent experiences without managing infrastructure.

title: Elevate your developer productivity with Windows Terminal
sortTitle: elevate your developer productivity with windows terminal
description: Developers lose time switching tools to maintain UNIX habits. This session shows how new improvements coming to Windows Terminal support consistent command‑driven workflows so developers can code with reduced context switching. Attendees learn how to create reusable workflows to help build and ship faster using familiar UNIX like workflows.

title: Expanding horizons: Foundry Local for Android and Edge devices
sortTitle: expanding horizons: foundry local for android and edge devices
description: Discover how Foundry Local extends AI capabilities across multiple platforms in this demo session. Find out how to deploy and optimize models, integrate them with enterprise workflows, and explore best practices for cross-platform compatibility. Learn about real-world use cases that support scalable cloud-to-edge AI solutions from Windows, Mac, and Android, fostering innovation and operational efficiency.

title: Faster and smarter agents with Redis and Foundry
sortTitle: faster and smarter agents with redis and foundry
description: AI agents are only as powerful as the data and memory behind them. In this session, learn how Azure Managed Redis and Microsoft Foundry enable faster, more intelligent agents through high-performance in-memory data, vector search, and semantic caching that deliver the right context in milliseconds while reducing latency and cost. Learn practical patterns—spanning RAG, persistent memory, and event-driven workflows—to build production-ready agents with richer context and real-time reasoning.

title: Fire, forget, sleep. VS Code agents that run without you
sortTitle: fire, forget, sleep. vs code agents that run without you
description: You can only be in one place at once. Your agents don't have that problem. Copilot agents in VS Code run three ways — local for interactive pairing, background for isolated tasks in worktrees, and cloud for autonomous long-running jobs. See when to reach for each, how to hand off between them, and manage parallel sessions from a single view. Custom agents, subagent orchestration, and delegating a local plan to background or cloud execution are all on the table.

title: Foundry Local in Action: AI on Windows Devices
sortTitle: foundry local in action: ai on windows devices
description: Join us for a session on getting started with Foundry Local on Windows, where we'll cover best practices and tips to help you make the most of using it on Windows.

title: From silent failures to faster insights in Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: from silent failures to faster insights in microsoft foundry
description: AI agents fail quietly, latency creeps up, costs spike, tools error, quality degrades. We’ll show how to accelerate monitoring, debugging and optimization of your agents with Microsoft Foundry capabilities like AskAI. In this 15‑minute talk, we'll walk through how Foundry turns raw traces into actionable insights and intuitive visualizations, moving you from “what happened?” to “what do I do next?” across failures, latency, cost, tokens, and evaluator signals.

title: Get More Than Autocomplete: GitHub Copilot Workflows in Visual Studio
sortTitle: get more than autocomplete: github copilot workflows in visual studio
description: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio goes far beyond code completion. In this lightning talk, see how developers use Copilot across the full inner loop — from understanding unfamiliar code, to generating tests, debugging failures, and reviewing changes with confidence. We’ll highlight practical workflows that help teams move faster without losing control, all inside Visual Studio.

title: MCP does way more than you think
sortTitle: mcp does way more than you think
description: You've connected an MCP server or two. But MCP in GitHub Copilot goes a lot further than tool lookups — and it works across the editor, CLI, and cloud. This session demos the range: connecting Copilot to databases, APIs, and internal systems wherever you work, plus a first look at MCP Apps, where tool calls return real UI like dashboards and forms right in chat. All live, all demos, no slides.

title: Monetize apps and agents with Microsoft Marketplace
sortTitle: monetize apps and agents with microsoft marketplace
description: Learn how to build, publish, and monetize apps and agents with Microsoft Marketplace to reach millions of customers and drive scalable growth. Getting published on Marketplace surfaces your solutions in the Marketplace storefront and Microsoft products, such as Microsoft 365 Copilot and Azure, to reach customers directly in the flow of work. This lightning talk covers the start-to-finish publishing process and highlights benefits and incentives available from Microsoft for software developers.

title: Quick wins for AI model selection with Microsoft Foundry
sortTitle: quick wins for ai model selection with microsoft foundry
description: With hundreds of AI models available, choosing and integrating the right one can be daunting. This lightning talk shares practical tips for evaluating, benchmarking, and adopting models using Microsoft Foundry’s streamlined tools. Learn how IDE integration and validation workflows help you start projects quickly and avoid common pitfalls.

title: Reliable AI for code and knowledge work with Claude
sortTitle: reliable ai for code and knowledge work with claude
description: Prototypes impress, but production exposes the cracks - inconsistent results across large codebases, context limits on long documents, and reasoning that breaks down in multi-step tasks. This lightning talk shows how Claude in Microsoft Foundry tackles these failure modes head-on. Learn practical patterns for handling long context, strengthening reasoning, and building resilient AI systems that hold up at scale.

title: Securing AI in production with Microsoft Defender for Cloud
sortTitle: securing ai in production with microsoft defender for cloud
description: As AI workloads move into production, security blind spots and cloud posture become critical risks. Learn how Microsoft Defender for Cloud secures AI workloads end‑to‑end—from infrastructure and data to AI services and agent‑based applications. Using a real‑world multi‑agent conversational platform as an example, the session shows how to identify risks, detect threats, and apply security best practices that enable teams to run AI systems in production with confidence and trust.

title: Simplify app dev with cloud-native PostgreSQL in Azure HorizonDB
sortTitle: simplify app dev with cloud-native postgresql in azure horizondb
description: Enterprise developers creating AI‑driven applications face growing complexity as vector search, models, and retrieval pipelines sprawl across services. In this  session, see how Azure HorizonDB streamlines the stack by embedding AI and search directly in the database. Learn to run hybrid vector queries, apply BM25 relevance, call managed AI models from SQL, and prototype agentic workflows—shipping faster with less architectural overhead.

title: Smarter training patterns for better AI models
sortTitle: smarter training patterns for better ai models
description: One-size-fits-all models rarely deliver top results. Discover how Foundry helps you get more from your data by offering advanced training patterns. Explore fine-tuning, reinforcement learning from traces, and powerful evaluation tools. Learn how observability, governance, and built-in productivity features speed up customization and cost reduction.

title: The agent factory mindset: Building AI apps the Foundry way
sortTitle: the agent factory mindset: building ai apps the foundry way
description: Microsoft Foundry reframes AI development as an agent factory—not a collection of one‑off demos. This talk explores how teams are designing AI apps around agents, tools, and workflows instead of traditional UI‑first patterns. Learn how Foundry enables agent composition, tool interoperability, and scalable deployment—so your AI apps behave more like systems and less like chatbots.

title: The developer experience with AKS Desktop and VS Code
sortTitle: the developer experience with aks desktop and vs code
description: Getting an app from local dev or on‑prem to AKS can be hard to make secure, repeatable, and easy to run. This session shows how AKS Desktop and VS Code speed the developer‑to‑AKS journey, from local Kubernetes workflows and containerisation to CI/CD and safe deployments, using built‑in Kubernetes best practices for reliable operations.

title: The Windows Security Features That Matter Most for Developers
sortTitle: the windows security features that matter most for developers
description: Windows security foundations are evolving at the OS level to support post‑quantum resilience and emerging agentic workflows.  We’ll cover what developers can start adopting now to help raise the security bar across the ecosystem while preserving application compatibility and minimizing friction.

title: Use agents to build WinUI3 apps
sortTitle: use agents to build winui3 apps
description: Learn how agent-based development workflows can be used to build modern Windows applications with WinUI 3. In this demo, we’ll showcase new platform capabilities to enable agents to assist with application design, architecture, implementation, and testing. See how developers can leverage these capabilities to create new WinUI 3 apps, improve existing applications, or migrate legacy apps to a modern Windows UI stack using intelligent automation across the development lifecycle.

title: VS Code features that would have saved you last week
sortTitle: vs code features that would have saved you last week
description: VS Code ships a lot of features and some of the best ones fly under the radar. This talk is a rapid-fire tour of the ones that don't make the headlines but save real time: sticky scroll, timeline view, built-in port forwarding, terminal intellisense, the merge editor, and more. No AI, no agents — just the editor being really good at its job.

title: What the shift from cloud apps to agentic systems means for developers
sortTitle: what the shift from cloud apps to agentic systems means for developers
description: AI is changing how applications and agents are built and operated. This lightning talk explains how agentic systems reason over context, take multi‑step actions, and adapt over time—and what that means for developers. We’ll cover how design, building, delivery, and operations evolve as apps become agent‑driven, and how Microsoft’s integrated agentic platform is able to support apps and agents responsibly in production.

title: Why GitHub Copilot misses context (and how to fix it)
sortTitle: why github copilot misses context (and how to fix it)
description: Out of the box, GitHub Copilot doesn't know your conventions, architecture opinions, or how you ship. But you can teach it. Set up shared rules and reusable prompts so Copilot follows your standards. Build custom agents and skills for workflows your team actually runs. Dial in settings that scale from five engineers to five hundred. Rules, skills, prompt files, custom agents — see how they fit and when to reach for each.
